name: Oman
summary: 2026-06-07 (Sunday): **REZAEI REASSERTED THE 'JOINT-MANAGEMENT-WITH-OMAN + TRANSIT-TOLL' PROPOSAL ATOP A STILL-THROTTLED STRAIT — RHETORIC, NO MECHANISM — WHILE THE PAKISTAN MEDIATION CHANNEL REACTIVATED IN THE BROADER ECOSYSTEM.** (intel A5/A6/B2) The iran-node companion trigger ('Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz toll mechanism publicly announced by 2026-06-05') EXPIRED un-met — only Rezaei's repeated rhetorical toll framing, no announced mechanism. The oman-node trigger (toll mechanism by 2026-06-13 OR US sanctions Oman) REMAINS WATCHING. Separately, Pakistan's interior minister Naqvi visited Iran Saturday for talks with Araghchi (REPORTED, Tasnim) — the mediation ecosystem (Pakistan formal + Oman/Qatar Gulf channels) is being worked even as the formal US-Iran table is suspended. Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **REZAEI REASSERTED THE HORMUZ 'JOINT-MANAGEMENT-WITH-OMAN' + TRANSIT-TOLL PROPOSAL — RHETORIC/POSITIONAL, NOT AN ACT; OMAN'S ROLE IS UNCHANGED-QUIET.** In the renewed-clash window, Iran's Rezaei reasserted the Hormuz 'sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-tolls' framing — positional RHETORIC on top of an already-throttled strait, NOT an announced mechanism. No permanent Oman-mediated toll mechanism has been published, so Rubio's red line is not breached and the Iran-Oman toll trigger stays watching (no act to encode). The 5-6 June overnight exchange (7-missile salvo at Kuwait/Bahrain, 4 drones toward Hormuz, US Goruk/Qeshm strike) ran around Oman, not through it; no fresh Oman mine alert surfaced. **No Oman trigger moves to active — Rezaei's framing is RHETORIC, not a mechanism announcement or a US sanction action.** PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **THE OMAN-MEDIATED HORMUZ-TOLL TRIGGER (~JUNE 5, ~48H OUT) IS LIKELY TO EXPIRE UNTRIPPED — IRAN'S HARDENED SUSPENSION OVERTOOK THE TOLL WORKSTREAM.** Iran's 3 June hardening of the talks suspension (Tasnim: 'no response given, text-exchange halted until Lebanon conditions met') freezes the Oman-mediated toll workstream the draft 60-day MOU contemplated; no permanent toll mechanism has been publicly announced, so Rubio's red line is not breached and the 'Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz toll mechanism within 14 days (by 2026-06-05)' trigger is on track to expire untripped in ~48h. Oman's role this window is unchanged-quiet: still the venue/facilitator and a US-Treasury sanction target over the toll question, with no fresh mine alert surfacing. The escalation (3 June barrage, US Qeshm strike) ran around Oman, not through it. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **NEW NODE — OMAN IS THE QUIET HINGE OF THE HORMUZ ENDGAME, AND NOW A POINT OF US FRICTION. Oman crossed the node-creation bar this window, appearing across ≥3 independent sources in four distinct causal roles: (1) HORMUZ MEDIATOR — the venue/facilitator for the US-Iran ceasefire-extension track and the proposed Oman-mediated Hormuz toll mechanism in the draft 60-day MOU; (2) MINE-ALERT AUTHORITY — Oman's Maritime Security Centre warned on 30 May of a floating object suspected to be a naval mine, sighted west of the Inshore Traffic Zone within Omani territorial waters, urging vessels to keep clear (CONFIRMED — operational/official); (3) TOLL-MECHANISM PARTY — Iran's desired permanent Hormuz toll mechanism is to be Oman-mediated, which Rubio's red line rejects and Hegseth's 'open, toll-free strait' demand directly opposes; (4) US TREASURY SANCTION TARGET — the US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction Oman if it imposes Hormuz transit tolls (REPORTED — single ToI liveblog headline, 28 May; needs corroboration). The Lian Star interdiction (6th blockade vessel) occurred in the Gulf of Oman. Net: Oman is simultaneously facilitating the deal and being strained by Washington over the toll question — friction between the US and its own mediator is new intelligence. This is a persistent entity with causal links to strait-of-hormuz, iran, united-states, qatar, and brent-crude.**

active_triggers.condition: Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz toll mechanism publicly announced within 14 days (by 2026-06-13) OR US sanctions Oman over tolls
active_triggers.note: Mirrors the iran-node Iran-Oman toll trigger. Rubio's red line rejects an Iran-Oman Hormuz toll mechanism; Hegseth demands an 'open, toll-free strait'; the US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction Oman over tolls (REPORTED, single headline). If a permanent toll mechanism is announced, Rubio's red line is breached; if the US sanctions its own mediator, the deal's mediation architecture is strained. No mechanism announced and no sanction imposed yet — watching.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-06: STAYS WATCHING. Rezaei's 5-6 June reassertion of the Hormuz 'joint-management-with-Oman + transit-toll' proposal is RHETORIC/positional — NOT a published permanent mechanism and NOT a US sanction action. Neither limb of the condition is met; no move.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-07: 2026-06-07: STAYS WATCHING — Rezaei reasserted the Oman-joint-management/toll proposal but NO mechanism announced; the companion iran-node trigger (by 06-05) EXPIRED un-met. US-Treasury-sanctions-Oman limb also not fired (intel A5/A6).

top_edges.context: 2026-06-06: Rezaei reasserted the Hormuz 'sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-toll' framing — RHETORIC/positional, NOT a mechanism announcement. Modest re-activation. weight = freq(3) x recency(1.0) x directness_mult(1.0) = 3.0 (held).

recent_signals.headline: A cargo ship was attacked off the Omani coast (Thu ~25 Jun), attacker unconfirmed — fresh risk to the Hormuz/Gulf of Oman approaches amid the IRGC enforcement. REPORTED.

The Omani navy is physically RESCUING crews from US-struck tankers in its waters (Settebello survivors; a Guinea-Bissau-flagged tanker hit by 2 Hellfires, 20 crew evacuated). The quiet mediator is now in direct OPERATIONAL contact with the blockade's consequences off its coast -- a node to watch as the enforcement campaign turns lethal near Omani waters.

**REZAEI REASSERTED THE HORMUZ OMAN-JOINT-MANAGEMENT + TOLL PROPOSAL (RHETORIC, NO MECHANISM); PAKISTAN'S NAQVI VISITED IRAN, MEDIATION ECOSYSTEM ALIVE.** Iran's Rezaei renewed the 'sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-toll' line atop an already-throttled strait (~10 ships/day vs ~95) — no new physical act and no announced toll mechanism; the iran-node 06-05 toll trigger EXPIRED un-met (intel A5/A6). The oman-node toll trigger (by 06-13 or US sanctions Oman) stays watching. Separately, Pakistan's Naqvi visited Iran Saturday for talks with Araghchi (REPORTED, Tasnim) — the back-channel ecosystem is being worked during the deadlock (intel B2).
recent_signals.verification: RHETORIC (toll proposal); REPORTED (Naqvi visit, single-source); CONFIRMED (Hormuz throttle ongoing)

recent_signals.headline: **REZAEI REASSERTED THE HORMUZ 'JOINT-MANAGEMENT-WITH-OMAN' + TRANSIT-TOLL PROPOSAL — RHETORIC/POSITIONAL, NOT AN ACT.** In the renewed-clash window, Iran's Rezaei reasserted the Hormuz 'sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-tolls' framing on top of an already-throttled strait. No permanent Oman-mediated toll mechanism has been published and no new US sanction on Oman has been imposed, so Rubio's red line is not breached. The 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange (7-missile salvo at Kuwait/Bahrain, 4 drones toward Hormuz, US Goruk/Qeshm strike) ran around Oman, not through it; no fresh Oman mine alert surfaced. Oman's role this window is unchanged-quiet.
sources: IRGC / Rezaei — Hormuz sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-toll framing (Iran rhetoric, positional)
recent_signals.verification: Rezaei Hormuz joint-management-with-Oman + toll framing = RHETORIC/positional (Iran statement, NOT a mechanism announcement, not elevated); no permanent toll mechanism published + no US sanction on Oman imposed = CONFIRMED non-events; Gulf exchange ran around Oman = CONFIRMED. No Oman trigger moves to active.

recent_signals.headline: **NODE CREATED — OMAN IS THE HORMUZ MEDIATOR, MINE-ALERT AUTHORITY, TOLL-MECHANISM PARTY, AND A NEW US SANCTION TARGET.** Oman's Maritime Security Centre warned on 30 May of a floating object suspected to be a naval mine within Omani territorial waters (west of the Inshore Traffic Zone), urging vessels to keep clear. Oman is the venue for the proposed Oman-mediated Hormuz toll mechanism in the draft 60-day MOU — which Rubio's red line rejects and Hegseth's 'toll-free strait' demand opposes — and the US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction Oman over tolls. The Lian Star interdiction (6th blockade vessel) occurred in the Gulf of Oman. Oman is simultaneously facilitating the deal and being strained by Washington over the toll question.
sources: Oman Maritime Security Centre advisory 30 May — operational/official
Milli Chronicle / ABC News live (corroborating the Oman advisory)
Times of Israel liveblog 28 May (US Treasury-Oman tolls) — REPORTED, single headline
Iran International liveblog 30 May (Lian Star, Gulf of Oman)
recent_signals.verification: Suspected-mine advisory = CONFIRMED ACTION (Oman MSC operational/official); Oman as ceasefire/Hormuz mediator + toll-mechanism venue = CONFIRMED (multi-source); US Treasury sanction threat over tolls = REPORTED (single ToI headline, needs corroboration); Lian Star in Gulf of Oman = REPORTED-to-CONFIRMED. Meets node-creation bar: ≥3 independent sources, persistent entity (country), causal links to strait-of-hormuz/iran/united-states/qatar/brent-crude.
